reason: The statement applies because the text describes a vision where the prophet Ezekiel is shown a valley of dry bones, which represent the whole house of Israel. The bones are described as dried up with lost hope, symbolizing a captive and desolate state. The prophecy speaks of restoration, bringing life back to the bones, symbolizing the return of the Hebrew people from captivity and being brought back to their own land. The mention of the whole house of Israel being in a state of despair and being brought back to their land aligns with the statement provided.
